name: Wembley Stadium
summary: The home of English football and the scene of its greatest triumph and its greatest heartbreaks: the 1966 World Cup final, the Euro 1996 semi-final, and the Euro 2020 final all played out beneath its arch.

name: Stadio delle Alpi
summary: Now demolished and replaced by the Allianz Stadium, this was the setting for the 1990 World Cup semi-final and Gazza's tears.

name: Luzhniki Stadium
summary: Russia's national stadium, where England's 2018 World Cup run ended against Croatia in extra time.

name: Al Bayt Stadium
summary: Designed to resemble a traditional Bedouin tent, this stadium hosted England's tight quarter-final defeat to eventual champions France in 2022.

name: Olympiastadion
summary: A stadium steeped in Olympic and footballing history, and the venue for England's second successive European Championship final defeat in 2024.

name: Estadio Azteca
summary: One of football's great cathedrals, host of two World Cup finals, and the venue for England's dramatic round-of-16 win over co-hosts Mexico in 2026.

name: Hard Rock Stadium
summary: Home of the Miami Dolphins, converted for football and the setting for England's extra-time quarter-final win over Norway in 2026.

name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
summary: A retractable-roof stadium hosting several 2026 World Cup matches, including England's round-of-32 win over DR Congo and their semi-final against Argentina.
